name: Symbiotic Nutrition Process
description: Symbiotic Nutrition Process in Multiple. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A35[Host Organism] --> B35[Symbiont Association]
    C35[Symbiont] --> B35
    B35 --> D35[Nutrient Exchange]
    D35 --> E35[Host Benefits]
    D35 --> F35[Symbiont Benefits]
    E35 --> G35[Enhanced Nutrition]
    F35 --> H35[Protected Environment]
    G35 --> I35[Growth Advantage]
    H35 --> I35
    
    style A35 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style C35 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B35 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style D35 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E35 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style F35 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style G35 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style H35 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style I35 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Symbiotic Nutrition Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: Metabolic interplay between hosts and their symbiotic microbes
sources.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

sources.title: The legume-rhizobium symbiosis: plant perspective on symbiotic nitrogen fixation
sources.authors: Masson-Boivin, C; Sachs, T; Hartmann, A; Vivant-Corjon, B.
sources.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
